title: Data Structures
description: Abstract data types and their concrete implementations: stacks, queues, linked lists, trees and hash tables, and the operations and use-cases that distinguish them.
objectives: Distinguish an abstract data type (ADT) from a concrete implementation
Implement and trace a stack (LIFO) and a queue (FIFO), and describe real uses for each
Explain the node/pointer concept behind a singly-linked list and trace traversal, insertion and deletion
Define binary tree terminology and the binary search tree (BST) property, and trace pre-order, in-order and post-order traversals
Explain how hashing maps keys to array indices, how collisions are handled, and why lookup is O(1) on average
